title: atriplex prostrata subsp. polonica
intro: Atriplex prostrata subsp. polonica, also known as Polish orach, is a subspecies of the Atriplex prostrata plant, a type of succulent shrub belonging to the amaranth family (Amaranthaceae). Traditionally, Polish orach has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in nutrients and have been used to treat conditions such as gastrointestinal problems, wounds, and skin irritations, thanks to their soothing and regenerative properties. Polish orach can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its health benefits to be harnessed and utilized in a variety of ways.
